tax: Kingdom of active divine powers. Distinguished from Celestials and Fiends by worship-mediated authority rather than planar species descent, and from Archfey by portfolio office rather than faerie sovereignty alone.
ap: Deities manifest through chosen iconography: a smith-god as heated iron hands and hammer-shadow, a cavern-mother as a robed giantess of limestone milk, a war-power as a body made of banners, wounds, and bronze. Avatars may be slain without exhausting the deity.
eco: They persist through cults, temples, vows, names, relics, divine realms, sacred stories, and office succession. A deity can weaken when forgotten but may survive as a vestige if the portfolio remains needed by the world.
beh: Deities behave according to portfolio and worship. They bargain in commandments, answer through signs, and punish violation of sacred contracts. Even benevolent deities can be dangerous when their office is invoked improperly.
traitsText: Traits include prayer response, consecration, avatar projection, divine senses within portfolio, resistance to mundane death, and the ability to mark mortals as priests, champions, or taboo-breakers.
abilities: Abilities include miracles, blessings, curses, avatar manifestation, sanctified terrain, oath-binding, prophecy, healing, smiting, and portfolio-specific interventions such as storm-calling, harvest renewal, or death-denial.
bg: Deities appear in the catalogue because the Throat contains cult practice, divine absences, sacred fossils, and Nadir worship that cannot be responsibly filed as ordinary Outsider biology.
g: Deities are portfolio-bearing divine entities with active worship or cosmic office. They are the clearest members of the deific domain and include gods whose power can project avatars, answer prayers, mark sacred ground, and bind oaths across distance.
note: Expanded branch: Deities now includes Major Deities, Minor Deities, Demigods, and Divine Vestiges.
